en.text: O Arjuna, descendant of the Kuru dynasty
O Madhusūdan, slayer of the enemy
It is not a fact that
actions of the senses engaging with material sense objects
it remains steady, its water never crossing the shore
even at the time of death
Such a conclusion, i.e., of the nature
it is inappropriate to grieve for it
and immeasurable, due to his extremely subtle nature
as an open door to heaven
it is an obstacle to the attainment of heaven
of that person bereft of self-control
and the situation after death is again unknown
imperceptible due to its extremely subtle nature
the situation before birth is unknown
of the irresolute – those who nurture mundane desires
For such a person devoid of this intelligence
his eyes brimming with tears, showing his distress
have been the object of great honour
and lead to enjoyment and opulence
Of those persons attached to enjoyment and opulence
meditation on the Lord, purity of thought
liberation from matter and entrance into the divine plane
for the brāhmaṇ who is in full knowledge that devotion to the Lord is the sole purport of the Vedas
the attainment of yet another body
bewildered in determining my duty and non-duty
is to perform your prescribed duties
this is the state of realising Brahma, the Absolute
by killing our teachers and superiors
sense-controlled Arjuna, the conqueror of sleep
birth, for undergoing the reactions to his past actions
and attaining liberation from the bondage of birth
remain awake (experience the pleasures and pains, sorrows and delusions of worldly life)
motivated by the fruits of action
How does he be and act
and they advocate the various ceremonies and rituals
so what cause is there for grief
Their hearts filled with selfish desires
Losing heart, thus losing the courage of the warrior by apprehending the fall of the dynasty
like the limbs of a tortoise
whose heart was filled with pity
for a kṣatriya, or one who upholds the law
you will be held the object of ridicule
from the great fear of birth and death in the material world
Great warriors like Duryodhan and others
the person absorbed in consciousness of Me, devoted to Me
material contact – the engagement of the senses with their objects
and for one who is dead
we do not wish to even live
is freed from the delusion of mundane existence
free from the false ego of ‘I’ and ‘mine’ in relation to the body and its attachments (having a divine relationship with the Supreme)
Give up duality (honour and dishonour, etc., in the purview of these modes)
transcendental to the three modes of material nature
be always situated in the truth (or remain with the true devotees)
steadily absorbed in consciousness of the Supreme
who sees (the futility of mundane endeavours and remains indifferent to the dualities)
Those motivated by the fruits of their actions
enter to be enjoyed (for they cannot disturb his mind)
falls into saṁsāra, the cycle of birth and death in the material world
momentarily please the ear (which, thus taken out of context, are effectively poisonous as they do not help the spiritual progress of the soul)
but not the taste to enjoy them
free from attraction and repulsion, adopting yukta-vairāgya or devotionally integrated detachment
By seeing with an attitude of equanimity
by a large expanse of water, a lake
in dedication to the Supreme Lord
one in samādhi, absorbed in thought of the Supreme
That which is night for all living beings (who, immersed in ignorance, remain asleep to spiritual knowledge)
reaching all planes, migratory through all species of life according to its actions
by the prayers described in all the Vedas for worshipping the various demigods
How does such a person of steady mind speak
of a person of firmly established wisdom
by the seers of the truth
by those flowery words of the Vedas
ready for Your counsel and direction
deal with the modes of material nature – worldly subjects
who are fond of interpreting the Vedas, not knowing the true directive of the Vedas
the person who can control his mind at will – whose mind is obedient to him
and whose hankering for pleasure has gone
the period between birth and death is known
who is free from attachment, fear, and anger
engaging in this buddhi-yoga, yoga of wisdom that I teach you
even if the body is destroyed
by the apparent contradictions of the Vedas
produces cold and heat, pleasure and pain sensations

ru.text: о том, о чем скорбеть не стоит
кто не обрел связь с Господом
духовная реальность за пределами рождения и смерти
просветленный разум, не привязанный к плодам трудов
разум, направленный на осознание собственной души и Всевышнего
просветленный разум, направленный на осознание себя душой и на осознание Всевышнего
о тех, кто расстался с жизнью
не стремясь приобретать и сохранять материальное
постоянно пребывающий в осознании собственной духовной природы и Всевышнего
